channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The Latest: Trio of crises looms over the campaign's final stretch
item.description: In a debate that evoked a calmer era in American politics, Tim Walz and JD Vance went after each other’s running mates Tuesday and sought to shore up their campaigns’ vulnerabilities at a time of renewed fears of a regional war in the Middle East and sadness over devastation from Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prosecutors lay out new evidence in Trump election case, accuse him of having 'resorted to crimes'
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump laid the groundwork to try to overturn the 2020 election even before he lost, knowingly pushed false claims of voter fraud and “resorted to crimes” in his failed bid to cling to power, according to a court filing unsealed Wednesday that offers new evidence from the lan
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump blasts DOJ for 'election interference,' calls Jack Smith case a 'scam' after judge unseals key filing
item.description: Former President Trump blasted the DOJ for having “disobeyed their own rule in favor of complete and total election interference" after a key filing from Special Counsel Jack Smith’s election case against him was unsealed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Walz explains 'friends with shooters' gaffe from the VP debate with Vance
item.description: Sen. JD Vance, R-Ohio, and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz on Wednesday traded barbs over Tuesday night's vice presidential debate during campaign stops in Pennsylvania and Michigan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mayorkas warns FEMA doesn't have enough funding to last through hurricane season
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Federal Emergency Management Agency can meet immediate needs but does not have enough funding to make it through the hurricane season, Homeland Security Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas told reporters Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden won't support a strike on Iran nuclear sites as Israel weighs response to Iran missile attack
item.description: JOINT BASE ANDREWS, Md. (AP) — President Joe Biden said Wednesday he will not support an Israeli strike on sites related to Tehran’s nuclear program in response to Iran's missile attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: JD Vance again refuses to say Donald Trump lost the 2020 presidential election
item.description: Republican vice presidential candidate JD Vance on Wednesday again refused to say who he believed won the 2020 presidential election, which has been the persistent focus of false claims by his running mate, Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Anti-abortion leaders undeterred as Trump for the first time says he'd veto a federal abortion ban
item.description: Anti-abortion leaders say they're undeterred by Donald Trump's comments that he would veto a federal abortion ban.
item.dc:creator: Christine Fernando, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump ups his ante with September fundraising haul
item.description: Former President Trump rakes in over $160 million in fundraising in September, up from his August haul, as he tries to catch up to Vice President Kamala Harris in the 2024 election cash dash.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Voters have largely positive response to exchange between Vance and Walz on shootings
item.description: Voters from all groups had a mostly positive response to Ohio Sen. JD Vance and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz's exchange on school shootings and gun control during the debate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DHS warns of risks around Oct. 7, November election in annual threat assessment
item.description: “We are, of course, aware that that anniversary may add even more fuel to an already challenging and heightened threat environment,” a DHS official said.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Cybersecurity head says there's no chance a foreign adversary can change US election results
item.description: The head of the U.S. government's cybersecurity agency says security for the nation’s election systems has become so robust that Russia, Iran or any other foreign adversary won't be able to alter the outcome of this year’s presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Christina A. Cassidy And Ali Swenson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harris dodges answering whether athletes should stand during national anthem in unearthed interview
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris did not answer directly whether athletes should stand during the national anthem, an unearthed media interview in 2016 shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bipartisan California lawmakers urge House leaders to boost disaster relief funding
item.description: A bipartisan group of lawmakers from Orange County, Calif., are urging House leaders to include disaster relief funding in fiscal 2025 spending, highlighting wildfire damage in the state even as damage from Hurricane Helene on the other side of the country has heightened attention on the issue.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State of Democra-Z: Takeaways from the vice presidential debate
item.description: The vice presidential debate is not expected to push many voters to one side or another, but it offered a closer look at the men who could be second in command. We check in with two Gen-Z voters about what stood out to them.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Swing state Dems ramping up ground game for final election push: 'Folks are responding'
item.description: The North Carolina Democratic Party shared with Fox News Digital that they are taking a community-based approach to their 2024 election ground game efforts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hurricane Helene renews calls for Congress to pass disaster funding
item.description: The widespread devastation wrought by Hurricane Helene is upping the pressure on lawmakers for a looming end-of-year fight over disaster funding.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Best and worst debate moments for Vance, Walz
item.description: Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) engaged in a policy-focused debate Tuesday, civilly sparring on immigration, abortion and the economy, among other issues.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Vance-Walz VP debate
item.description: Ohio Sen. JD Vance (R) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) met face-to-face in New York City on Tuesday in the first and likely only vice presidential debate of the election cycle, a high-stakes moment that comes a little more than a month before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After pressing an Israel-Hezbollah cease-fire, the Biden administration shifts its message
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration says there is a significant difference between Israeli actions that have expanded its war against the Iranian-backed militant groups Hamas and Hezbollah and Iran’s retaliatory missile attack against Israel, which it condemned as escalatory.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tennessee plastics factory employee recounts narrow escape through floodwater
item.description: Impact Plastics employee Zinna Adkins stepped outside her workplace at 10:54 a.m. on Friday and captured water rising in the parking lot on her cellphone. But at that time, she said no supervisor informed employees they were free to leave.
item.dc:creator: Karen Jenkins, Fox 5

item.title: Breaking down the CD-22 race and its candidates ahead of high-stakes Wednesday debate
item.description: Kicking it off Wednesday evening is the head-to-head between Republican incumbent David Valadao and Democrat Rudy Salas for California's 22nd Congressional District.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson says Congress has provided funds for Helene response
item.description: “Congress has previously provided the funds it [FEMA] needs to respond, so we will make sure that those resources are appropriately allocated,” Johnson said.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump takes lead over Harris in North Carolina Quinnipiac survey
item.description: The poll offers much better news for Trump than last month’s poll from Quinnipiac, which found Harris leading Trump by 3 points.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Habitat for Humanity volunteers continue Carter’s legacy on 100th birthday
item.description: For 40 years, the project has brought together volunteers, celebrities and elected officials to build homes and highlight the need for affordable housing.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Why Tuesday's vice presidential debate could matter more than history suggests
item.description: Tim Walz and JD Vance might have the chance to reshape the political landscape Tuesday in their first and only debate.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Reviewing the Vance-Walz vice-presidential debate
item.description: MPR News host Angela Davis talks with political analysts about Tuesday night’s vice-presidential debate between Ohio Sen. JD Vance and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Cari Dwyer, MPR News

item.title: Vance and Walz are both veterans. Here's what it means to have a vet VP
item.description: For the first time since 1996, both major parties have picked a veteran for vice president — a fact that some veterans' groups are hoping it leads to greater understanding of military and vet issues.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence, MPR News

item.title: How young Republicans and Democrats are whipping up votes in key districts
item.description: As the presidential race ramps up in Georgia, one vital voting demographic is mobilizing and hoping to impact the race: young people.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly, Erika Ryan, Kira Wakeam, and Courtney Dorning, MPR News

item.title: Austrian far-right party wins national vote but its chances of governing are unclear
item.description: The Freedom Party on Sunday secured the first far-right national parliamentary election victory in post-World War II Austria. But political rivals have said they won’t work with the party’s leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: 5 people killed in a 4-vehicle chain reaction crash on central Utah highway
item.description: Four people from Minnesota and a Utah man were killed in a four-vehicle chain reaction crash in central Utah. The Utah Highway Patrol says an SUV pulling onto U.S. Highway 6 near Price overshot the acceleration lane and pulled into the path of a semitractor pulling double trailers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Harris trolls Trump at Vegas rally and LA fundraiser, says her crowds are 'pretty big'
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris poked at Donald Trump over crowd sizes, his refusal to debate again and his privileged background on Sunday as she hauled in campaign cash in California and held a raucous rally at the same Nevada venue where the Republican nominee had appeared just two
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Prop. 36 faces intense review as North County residents gather for informative session
item.description: Voters have a lot to consider this upcoming election when it comes to Proposition 36, a statewide ballot measure that seeks a tougher approach on crime.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Franco, Fox 5
